page: Microsoft Azure E64ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epsv6 server family. It is powered by a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 64 dedicated physical cores running at 3.4 GHz. The architecture features a single thread per core, providing predictable compute performance. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes 128 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and 64 KB of L1d cache. No local storage is bundled, though the instance is Premium Storage capable, and there are no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core performance and exceptional memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation tasks. Memory bandwidth is highly optimized, reaching top-tier performance in both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached read/write operations. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. Single-core performance, single-threaded decompression, and LLM prompt processing fall into the average tier, indicating that workloads must be highly parallelized to fully exploit the hardware.

Utilizing ARM-based architecture allows this server to deliver high resource density and memory capacity with favorable qualitative cost efficiency compared to traditional x86 memory-optimized instances. The absence of hyperthreading ensures dedicated execution resources per vCPU, which is highly beneficial for predictable execution. This profile makes the E64ps_v6 highly suitable for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads such as large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized software compilation.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64ps_v6 is an ARM-based, memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a 64-core Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. Featuring a dedicated single-thread-per-core design and 512.0 GB of RAM, it provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. While it lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database and Redis throughput. Conversely, single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile delivers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, high-throughput web serving, and concurrent software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 512 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, making it ideal for large-scale databases and in-memory caches.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64ps_v6 is a memory-optimized ARM virtual machine with 64 dedicated cores, 512 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core ARM virtual machine delivering high memory density and top-tier multi-threaded performance for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core ARM Neoverse-N2 processor at 3.4 GHz
Equipped with 512 GB of RAM (8 GB per core) for memory-intensive tasks
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis performance
Dedicated single-thread-per-core architecture with no hyperthreading
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Optimized for large databases, in-memory caching, and web serving
